coupling.note: Adding DurationInput to Def is incremental (already imports Effect). But retry? is a runtime concern leaking into a definition interface — coupling tool definition with execution policy.

debuggability.note: Span enrichment is good (tool.type). But Effect.timeout produces bare TimeoutError with no tool name. Developer sees 'Timed out' with no context of which tool or arguments. Score kept at 2 — below the 3 minimum required for approval.

convergence.note: Moving toward better concurrency control with read/write split. Aligns with target architecture. Downside: no retry implementation yet, but that's deferred correctly.

surface_area.note: Adding 3 optional fields to public Def interface is moderate surface. retry? is especially risky as dead surface — external plugin tool authors could set it expecting retry behavior that doesn't exist.

testability.note: Semaphore split is testable. Per-tool semaphore Map is harder to test because it's scoped inside resolve(). Effect.timeout on tool.execute is testable in isolation. Per-tool semaphore lifecycle is hard to verify in a 10-line script.

error_clarity.note: Timeout errors propagate as bare TimeoutError. No custom typed error wrapping tool ID, no span attribution on the timeout error path. span enrichment partially mitigates this but only for the success path.

reversibility.note: 2 files, both dirty with other lane work. Adding optional fields to Def is independently revertible. tools.ts changes (semaphore split + config propagation) are tightly coupled — hard to revert one without the other. MCP section changes are entangled because chooseSemaphore doesn't handle MCP types.

blocking_objections.finding: chooseSemaphore uses Semaphore.make(item.maxConcurrency) which returns Effect<Semaphore>, not Semaphore. The helper function signature `(): Semaphore.Semaphore` is incompatible with `Semaphore.make()` which returns `Effect<Semaphore>`. Must use Semaphore.makeUnsafe(item.maxConcurrency).
blocking_objections.repair: Replace Semaphore.make with Semaphore.makeUnsafe in the chooseSemaphore helper. Import makeUnsafe from effect/Semaphore.

blocking_objections.finding: retry?: number added to Tool.Def interface but never consumed in tools.ts. No retry logic exists. This is dead surface area on the public interface — any tool setting retry: 3 will silently ignore it, creating false expectations for external consumers and plugin tool authors.
blocking_objections.repair: Remove retry?: number from Tool.Def. File a follow-up issue to implement retry as a separate, complete lane that both consumes and tests the field.

blocking_objections.finding: MCP section type mismatch: chooseSemaphore checks item.cacheable and item.maxConcurrency which don't exist on MCP tool objects. MCP tools have structure { execute, inputSchema, ... } not Tool.Def. Applying chooseSemaphore(item) in the MCP section causes TypeScript type errors.
blocking_objections.repair: For the MCP section, either: (1) keep using writeSemaphore unconditionally, (2) add a type guard before chooseSemaphore, or (3) create a separate chooseMcpSemaphore that maps by string key and defaults to writeSemaphore.

blocking_objections.finding: Span enrichment mentions 'semaphore.available': Semaphore.availablePermits(...) — but no such API exists in Effect v4's Semaphore. The Semaphore interface only exposes take(), release(), releaseAll(), resize(), withPermit[s]. There is no non-destructive read-only getter for available permits.
blocking_objections.repair: Remove the 'semaphore.available' span attribute from the plan. Alternatively, implement a Semaphore wrapper that tracks available permits via a Ref<number> updated on take/release. File as a follow-up.

significant_concerns.finding: Effect.timeout produces Cause.TimeoutError with generic 'Timed out' message. The existing Effect.catch handler passes this to Execution.publishFailed and re-fails it. No tool name, tool ID, or timeout duration is included in the error. Developer debugging a timeout issue sees no indication of WHICH tool timed out.
significant_concerns.repair: Add Effect.catchTag or Effect.mapError before the catch-all to convert TimeoutError into a typed error that includes tool.id and item.timeout, e.g. ToolTimeoutError({ tool: item.id, duration: item.timeout }).

significant_concerns.finding: tool.ts and tools.ts are both dirty (other lanes' ongoing work). tool.ts already has cacheable? and R type param from other lanes. Phase 2 Parallel Dispatch already added Semaphore import and semaphore.withPermit wrapping in tools.ts. Lane 0d must target search_replace precisely to avoid removing other lanes' code.
significant_concerns.repair: Use targeted search_replace with expected_before_sha256 on each edit. Pre-read both files' current dirty state at edit time, not at plan time. Target specific insertion points (e.g., add fields after cacheable? in Def interface).

non_blocking_observations.finding: perToolSemaphores Map has no cleanup path, but resolve() is called per-request (from prompt.ts line 1387), so the Map is scoped to a single request. No cross-request leak. Only a concern if dynamic MCP tools change within a single resolve() call.
non_blocking_observations.recommendation: Document as known scoping behavior. No action needed for current lane.

non_blocking_observations.finding: Effect.timeout is scoped to item.execute(args, ctx) only, not the full execution including plugin triggers (plugin.trigger, Execution.publishStarted). If a plugin hook hangs, the timeout won't help.
non_blocking_observations.recommendation: Consider whether timeout should wrap the entire semaphore-withPermit block including plugin hooks. If yes, move the wrapper outside the inner scope.

non_blocking_observations.finding: DurationInput type import needs verification. tool.ts imports from 'effect' but DurationInput is in 'effect/Duration'. Check if Effect re-exports it or if a second import path is needed.
non_blocking_observations.recommendation: Verify with `bun run typecheck` during implementation. May need `import type { DurationInput } from 'effect/Duration'`.

follow_up_issues.title: Implement retry logic for Tool.Def.retry field
follow_up_issues.description: retry?: number was removed from this lane because it was dead surface area. A future lane should add both the field AND the retry-with-Schedule implementation in tools.ts.

follow_up_issues.title: Implement Semaphore available-permits tracking for spans
follow_up_issues.description: Effect v4 Semaphore has no non-destructive available-permits getter. Build a tracked Semaphore wrapper (Ref<number> + take/release interceptor) for span enrichment with current permit pressure.
